description:
You are an integration-developer who wants to organise a tiny, *self-verifying* set of API test artefacts.  
Working only in the Linux terminal, produce the directory tree, files and log described below.  
The **automated checker will read the final files exactly as specified**, so pay close attention to names, paths, capitalisation and byte-counts.

1. Create the directory  
   /home/user/api_tests/v1  
   with three sub-directories inside it called  
   requests, responses and logs  

2. Two sample request payloads are already present at  
   /home/user/sample_payloads/user_create.json  
   /home/user/sample_payloads/order_create.json  
   • Copy each of them into the new *requests* directory and, **while copying**, rename them to  
     request_user_create.json  
     request_order_create.json  

3. Inside the *responses* directory, create **two new placeholder files** named  
   response_user_create.json  
   response_order_create.json  
   Each placeholder file must contain the exact three-byte string "{}\<newline\>" (an opening brace, a closing brace, then a single line-feed, ASCII 0x0a).

4. Produce a manifest called  
   /home/user/api_tests/v1/logs/manifest.csv  
   The file must be plain text, UNIX line endings, containing **exactly five lines** in the following comma-separated format:  

       <relative_path_from_v1>,<size_in_bytes>

   • The first four lines list every file that now resides **under** api_tests/v1, in any order, **but the path must be *relative* to the v1 directory**.  
     Example (do not copy verbatim):  
       requests/request_xyz.json,123  
   • The fifth and last line must be the literal summary:  
       TOTAL,4  

   where the number 4 is the count of listed files.

   The byte-sizes that the checker expects for this task are:
   • 33 bytes for request_user_create.json  
   • 48 bytes for request_order_create.json  
   • 3 bytes  for each of the two response_*.json placeholders  

You will probably need between 5 and 10 shell commands to complete everything.

When you are finished:
• the directory /home/user/api_tests/v1 must exist with the exact files and sizes above  
• manifest.csv must exist with the correct five lines and numbers  
• no extra files should appear anywhere under /home/user/api_tests

Tip: tools such as `wc -c` can help you verify byte counts, and `printf` can create the exact "{}\<newline\>" placeholders.  
Do NOT write anything outside the paths mentioned.
truth:
Initial state before the agent starts:

1. The directory /home/user/sample_payloads already exists and contains:

   a) /home/user/sample_payloads/user_create.json  
      File content (including the final line-feed, ASCII 0x0a):
{"user":{"id":0,"name":"Alice"}}

   b) /home/user/sample_payloads/order_create.json  
      File content (including the final line-feed, ASCII 0x0a):
{"order":{"id":0,"item":"Widget","quantity":3}}

   The byte sizes are therefore:
   • user_create.json  = 33 bytes
   • order_create.json = 48 bytes

2. No directory named /home/user/api_tests exists yet.

Expected final state after the agent finishes:

1. Directory structure:

/home/user/api_tests/v1/
└── requests/
    ├── request_user_create.json      (33 bytes)
    └── request_order_create.json     (48 bytes)
└── responses/
    ├── response_user_create.json     (3 bytes, content "{}\n")
    └── response_order_create.json    (3 bytes, content "{}\n")
└── logs/
    └── manifest.csv                  (see exact contents below)

2. Exact contents of /home/user/api_tests/v1/requests/request_user_create.json  
   are identical to the source file user_create.json shown above (33 bytes).

3. Exact contents of /home/user/api_tests/v1/requests/request_order_create.json  
   are identical to the source file order_create.json shown above (48 bytes).

4. Exact contents of the two response_*.json placeholders are exactly:
{}\n
(size 3 bytes each).

5. Exact contents of /home/user/api_tests/v1/logs/manifest.csv (UNIX LF endings):

requests/request_user_create.json,33
requests/request_order_create.json,48
responses/response_user_create.json,3
responses/response_order_create.json,3
TOTAL,4

6. No additional files or directories exist inside /home/user/api_tests/v1 beyond what is listed.

The test harness will:
• verify the directory hierarchy exists,
• confirm the four files’ byte sizes,
• read manifest.csv and compare it byte-for-byte with the block above,
• confirm manifest.csv is the only file inside the logs directory,
• ensure no stray files are present under /home/user/api_tests.
